commands.review_summary:
The PR moves WSL gateway keepalive startup, distro resolution, stale marker/process cleanup, and WSL process spawning from `App.xaml.cs` into a new `WslGatewayKeepAliveService`, with `App` delegating during launch.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR is a behavior-preserving refactor rather than a bug report. The relevant runtime path is the WSL keepalive startup, which the attached recording and log line demonstrate after the extraction.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed because there are no actionable review findings; the remaining action is normal maintainer merge judgment with CI and validation gates.

target.title: refactor: extract WslGatewayKeepAliveService from App.xaml.cs
target.body:
## Summary

`App.xaml.cs` carried the WSL gateway keepalive logic inline: a fire-and-forget task that keeps the local gateway's WSL2 VM alive after the tray exits, plus cleanup of stale keepalive processes and markers. This moves that concern into a dedicated `WslGatewayKeepAliveService`.

## What changed

- New `Services/WslGatewayKeepAliveService.cs` — holds the nine methods (~220 lines) that previously lived in `App`: the entry task, distro resolution, stale-process/marker cleanup, and the WSL process spawn.
- `App.xaml.cs` — the startup seam in `OnLaunchedAsync` now constructs the service and delegates; the original methods are removed.
- The two `App` fields the code reads, `_settings` and `_gatewayRegistry`, enter the service as `Func<>` delegates instead of direct field access. Nothing else changes — same logic, same ordering, same threading, same side effects.
- The keep-alive *window* (`_keepAliveWindow`, the WinUI `XamlRoot` anchor) is an unrelated concern and stays in `App`.

## Testing

- `./build.ps1`
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests.csproj --no-restore`
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests.csproj --no-restore`

No new tests: the decision logic is already covered by `WslKeepAlivePolicyTests` (unchanged); the moved methods are process/filesystem orchestration that was not unit-tested before and is not behavior-changed here.

## Proof

Manual run after the extraction. With the local WSL gateway active, the keepalive fires through the new service on startup, identical to the pre-refactor path:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b87c3f1d-5488-4f0a-aa1c-1a97afbe753b



```
[2026-05-30 19:13:09.126] [INFO] [WslKeepAlive] Started keepalive for OpenClawGateway (PID 40764).
```

## Notes

No behavior change is intended. The seam (`Task.Run` before `InitializeGatewayClient()`, off the UI thread), the WSL command (`wsl.exe -d <distro> -- sleep infinity`), distro resolution precedence, and the stale-cleanup path are all preserved exactly.

Addresses part of #554.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
target.author: alexalves87
